title: Amazon Linux 2 packages
body:
Hello,

Is there a way to get packages for Amazon Linux 2, please?
RHEL7 option from https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/install.md doesn't work because there is no such repository "rhel-7-server-extras-rpms" in Amazon Linux.

comments.body: were you able to determine any solution to this? I'm faced with the same problem.

@apg036 @cgeers I'm guessing you can use centos7 packages, yes? Could you try that first from the default centos repos? Also, RE: newer packages for CentOS, I'm working on sending Fedora rebuilds for CentOS to the kubic project repositories on OBS. Will update the docs when ready..

In case you wanna try a quick sample, you can get centos conmon package from kubic:libcontainers:testing repo. See: https://software.opensuse.org//download.html?project=devel%3Akubic%3Alibcontainers%3Atesting&package=conmon

buildah and others will follow..

@apg036 @cgeers ping, can you try this please:

```
cd /etc/yum.repos.d/
wget https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/CentOS_7/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo
yum install buildah
```

I'll do a doc update for centos7 and include amazon linux 2 in there if it works.

Did you confirm this actually works on amazon linux 2? When I run that command on a fresh instance, it fails on selinux dependencies. 

```
Error: Package: buildah-1.14.0-1.1.el7.x86_64 (devel_kubic_libcontainers_stable)
           Requires: container-selinux
```

This issue isn't unique to buildah. Docker 19 has the exact same problem. The solution there was to force install some packages from other repos.

@tophercullen sorry I did not, I just went by the assumption that whatever works on CentOS would work on it as well. See: https://github.com/containers/podman.io/issues/207#issuecomment-599591639 if that helps at all.

Amazon linux does have a 'extras' channel, but its a different notation entirely from rhel's 'extras' channel: https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2019/07/amazon-linux-2-extras-provides-aws-optimized-versions-of-new-linux-kernels/

On Mon, Mar 16, 2020 at 01:12:41PM -0700, tophercullen wrote:
> Amazon linux does have a 'extras' channel, but its a different notation entirely from rhel's 'extras' channel: https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2019/07/amazon-linux-2-extras-provides-aws-optimized-versions-of-new-linux-kernels/
> 

Is there any channel that provides rebuilds of RHEL's extras in that case?

-- 
Lokesh
IRC, GitHub: lsm5
GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
https://keybase.io/lsm5

Not to my knowledge. 

When I had to work around this for testing a PoC docker 19, I need to manually acquire packages from another repo and install them `rpm`. It got me past the dep requirements, but I didn't test full functionality to see if everything worked. Ended up going back to docker 18 which didn't require the extra sketchy steps. 

I can dig up those steps if you are interested.

@tophercullen hmm, would you mind trying this out please if you have a machine to spare?
Create a `/etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-Extras.repo`

```
[CentOS-Extras]
name=CentOS-$releasever - Extras
mirrorlist=http://mirrorlist.centos.org/?release=$releasever&arch=$basearch&repo=extras&infra=$infra
#baseurl=http://mirror.centos.org/centos/$releasever/extras/$basearch/
gpgcheck=0
```

followed by a `yum install buildah`

I'm not sure if the `$releasever` and other variables will work as-is, I can send an updated config if it doesn't.

Tried that initially as well, hoping it would be a better solution that what I did before (`$releasever` is 7 btw). It doesn't work. It just fails on the another downstream dependency.

@tophercullen hmm, I don't want to mess with CentOS users' environments, so I guess I'll create a COPR for this package and get back to you. Once we have tested this, we can update the Amazon docs.

@tophercullen please try this:
```
cd /etc/yum.repos.d/
sudo wget https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/CentOS_7/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo
sudo yum -y install yum-plugin-copr
sudo yum -y copr enable lsm5/container-selinux
sudo yum -y install buildah
```

This worked for me on an amazon linux container. Once you verify this, I can PR to update the install doc.
I tested this on an actual Amazon Linux 2 Instance and it works.
